title: taraxacum officinale subsp. vulgare
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. vulgare, commonly known as the common dandelion, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Taraxacum officinale subsp. vulgare include reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and supporting digestion, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or using a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
